title: bursera simaruba
intro: Bursera simaruba, also known as West Indian birch or West Indian rosewood, is a species of tree that belongs to the family Burseraceae. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Bursera simaruba include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne. Herbal preparations of Bursera simaruba include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
